title: Segmentation fault on Ubuntu 20.04
body:
This is a very bizarre bug that shows up intermittently. I'll do my best to explain what's going on here but feel free to clarify as needed.

When running the test suite for https://github.com/openhwgroup/cvw using Verilator, I get failures on certain distros with certain tests. On Ubuntu 22.04/24.04 and Rocky 9.3, Verilator runs without issues. On Ubuntu 20.04 and Rocky 8.9, I get an error message saying `Segmentation fault (core dumped)`. This does not happen with all of our tests, and there does not seem to be a hard and fast rule regarding which ones it does happen for, but I've noticed the following trends. Failures are more likely with larger configurations (though it has happened once or twice with a smaller configuration) and failures are more common the more tests have been run or are being run simultaneously. For example, with a new system, the first few tests work fine, and then by the 5th or 6th they usually start to fail. Then there are usually like 10 failures in a row before getting a few more random successes. I have to remove the compiled files and rerun Verilator on a clean repo to make it work for a few tests again.

For the cases that do fail, the compilation finishes (supposedly successfully), but when running the compiled file (Vtestbench) it immediately hits the segmentation fault. By adding `$display` statements, I have discovered that it is failing even before the first `initial begin` in the testbench.

I have installed and am using gcc-10 since the existing gcc-9 in Ubuntu 20.04 seems to be too old for Verilator.


Can you attach an example that shows the issue?  (Must be openly licensed, ideally in test_regress format.)

Due to the intermittent nature of the error, I have been unable to produce a simplified version for debugging, but I created a docker container with all of the tools installed. Download and run using the following commands:
```
docker pull ghcr.io/jordancarlin/cvw/ubuntu20cvw:latest
docker run -ti ubuntu20cvw:latest                       # add any extra volumes that you need for debugging
```
Inside the docker container, compile the tests and then either run all of the tests at once (most likely to give failures) or one test at a time using the commands below. These commands these tests run are all in the attached [Makefile](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/16327801/Makefile.txt) (which can also be seen at `home/wally/cvw/sim/verilator` in the docker container).

```
make riscof                               # compile all tests
regression-wally                          # run all tests
wsim --sim verilator rv64gc arch64zfh     # run one test using script
```

What 'verilator --version' are you using?  Did you try it with the git master version?
Primarily tested on the git master version. Also tried on 5.026

What OS and distribution are you using?
The issue is happening on Ubuntu 20.04 and Rocky 8.9. The same setup on Ubuntu 22.04/24.04 and Rocky 9 works fine.

May we assist you in trying to fix this in Verilator yourself?
I'm not familiar with the inner workings of Verilator, but I'm willing to give it a go if you think it would be easy for a beginner.
